repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes biographical material, correspondence, lectures, drafts of his writings, research notes, subject files and course material.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Includes biographical material, correspondence, lectures, drafts of his writings, research notes, subject files and course material.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Richard J. Schwartz Professor of Government Emeritus, a scholar of English and American political thought and history. Kramnick graduated summa cum laude from Harvard (1959), studied at Cambridge University (1959-60), earned his Ph.D. at Harvard (1965); then taught at Harvard, Brandeis and Yale before coming to Cornell in 1972. He was associate dean of the College of Arts and Sciences from 1986-89; led the Department of Government from 1996-2001; and served as the university's first vice provost for undergraduate education from 2001-05, during which time the New Student Reading Project was born. He retired in 2015. Kramnick was one of the founding members of the Faculty Fellows and Faculty-in-Residence programs that prioritized faculty-student interactions at Cornell and played the leading role in the development of the West Campus House System and its living-learning environment. He also taught at Cornell's Adult University. In 1998 he was elected a fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. At Cornell, he was a recipient of the Clark Distinguished Teaching Award and was named a Stephen H. Weiss Presidential Fellow.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Isaac Kramnick papers, #14-16-4416. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/66711

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials separated from the resource: Annotated copy of The Slected Political Writings of John Locke, ed. by Paul E. Sigmund (2005, W.W. Norton Company) cataloged separately. Handwritten notes removed from book and filed in box 7, folder 59.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files arranged chronologically except where noted otherwise.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folder titles were transcribed from Kramnick's original titles ecept where noted otherwise.

controlaccess.subject: Political science -- Great Britain -- History -- 18th century
Political science -- United States -- History -- 18th century
Universities and colleges -- Alumni and alumnae
Universities and colleges -- Faculty

did.unittitle: Nehru Centre of the High Commission of India Laski commemoration programme and photograph

PS 118: L. Hartz-Liberalism and Its Critics
NEH Summer Seminar for School Administrators
Freedom and Justice in the Western Tradition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research, notes, clippings, and drafts for writing projects and lectures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by subject. "American History" sub-series is original to Kramnick's papers, while "Cornell History" was devised by processing staff.
